Present course structure/syllabus information using the TrainingCourse.description field (and any structured content provided there) in a dedicated content area.
Provide a training registration form that captures full name, email, company, phone, and participants count, linked to the current courseId.
Validate registration form inputs on the client side (required fields, value types) before calling submitTrainingRegistration.
Submit training registrations using submitTrainingRegistration and display a success or error state using the returned success, registration, and message values.
Update page state after successful registration to show a confirmation panel while staying on training_detail.html, optionally reflecting this state in URL parameters.
Integrate the global search bar that calls globalSearch to search across machine models, spare parts, documents, and case studies, with search results presented without leaving the page.
user_workflows: View course details workflow: 1) User navigates to training_detail.html?courseId={id} from training_list.html or index.html. 2) On page initialization, the page-state-controller component reads courseId from the URL. 3) If courseId is present, training-detail-loader calls getTrainingCourseDetails(courseId). 4) On successful response, TrainingCourse data is passed to training-summary-panel, training-schedule-info, and training-description-block to render all course details. 5) If courseId is missing or getTrainingCourseDetails fails, an error state is shown instead of course details.

Register for a course workflow: 1) User reviews course details on the Training Detail page. 2) User scrolls to or focuses the training-registration-form. 3) User enters fullName and email (required), and optionally company, phone, and participantsCount. 4) On submit, the form validates required fields and value formats. 5) If validation passes, training-registration-form calls submitTrainingRegistration with courseId (from URL) and the form values. 6) While waiting, the form shows a loading state and temporarily disables the submit action. 7) If submitTrainingRegistration returns success=true, the form clears any error messages, and registration-confirmation-panel is populated with the returned TrainingRegistration and message. 8) The page-state-controller updates the URL to training_detail.html?courseId={id}&registrationStatus=success (and may include a registrationId from the returned registration) without full reload, and scrolls/focuses the confirmation panel. 9) If success=false, an error message is shown near the form using the returned message, and the user can correct input and resubmit.

Post-registration revisit workflow: 1) User returns to a URL such as training_detail.html?courseId={id}&registrationStatus=success. 2) On initialization, page-state-controller detects registrationStatus=success. 3) training-detail-loader still calls getTrainingCourseDetails(courseId) to render course information. 4) Because there is no interface to re-fetch a TrainingRegistration by ID, registration-confirmation-panel shows a generic success/‘already registered’ state for this course (without assuming detailed registration data) while still allowing the user to see course details.

Global search workflow: 1) User types a query into the global-search-bar input. 2) On submit, global-search-bar calls globalSearch with query and appropriate entityTypes (e.g., ["machine_models", "spare_parts", "documents", "case_studies"]). 3) While awaiting results, a loading indication is shown. 4) When results arrive, search-results-overlay displays machineModels, spareParts, documents, and caseStudies grouped by type. 5) If the user selects a non-training result, normal site navigation (outside this page spec) may redirect them; if they dismiss the overlay, they remain on the Training Detail page without affecting the course or registration state.
interactions: On page load or when the URL changes, the page-state-controller reads courseId and optional registrationStatus (and optional registrationId) URL parameters to determine which course to load and which panels to display.
If courseId is missing or invalid, an error message is shown and the registration form is disabled to prevent submissions without a valid course context.

Clicking or focusing the registration section control (for example, a 'Register' anchor or button tied to the registration form) scrolls or jumps the page to the training-registration-form component while preserving URL parameters.
Typing in any registration form field updates the component’s local form state; validation messages are dynamically shown or cleared as the user edits.
Submitting the registration form triggers client-side validation; if validation fails, the submit is cancelled and the form highlights invalid fields without calling submitTrainingRegistration.

If client-side validation passes, the form calls submitTrainingRegistration with courseId and current form field values, and sets a loading state that disables duplicate submissions until a response is received.

On successful registration submission (success=true), the registration-confirmation-panel becomes visible, displaying confirmation information based on the returned TrainingRegistration and message, and the page-state-controller updates the URL (e.g., adding registrationStatus=success and a registrationId) without a full reload.

On registration submission failure (success=false or network error), the training-registration-form shows an error message (derived from the submitTrainingRegistration response or generic error) and leaves the form values intact so the user can adjust and retry.

Using the global search input and submit action invokes globalSearch; the search-results-overlay appears over the page content with grouped results that the user can inspect or dismiss without interrupting the current course or form state.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary page state is controlled via URL parameters and in-memory component state. The required courseId parameter selects which TrainingCourse record to load via getTrainingCourseDetails. On initial load or whenever the URL changes (e.g., via browser navigation), the page-state-controller reads courseId from the query string: if present and non-empty, training-detail-loader calls getTrainingCourseDetails(courseId); if absent or if the call fails, the page enters an error state where course detail components are hidden or show an error banner, and the registration form is disabled. After a successful registration submission, the page-state-controller updates the URL to include registrationStatus=success and may include a registrationId corresponding to registration.id returned by submitTrainingRegistration. This update is done through history.pushState or history.replaceState so that the user remains on training_detail.html. On subsequent page loads, if registrationStatus=success is present, registration-confirmation-panel is initialized in a generic 'registration successful for this course' mode without assuming that detailed TrainingRegistration data can be re-fetched. The page does not use localStorage or other persistent client storage; all persistent state that affects what is shown is derived from URL parameters and freshly fetched API data (TrainingCourse via getTrainingCourseDetails, and registration state only from the in-session response of submitTrainingRegistration). Global search state (current query and results) is managed in memory within the global-search-bar and search-results-overlay components and is not encoded in the URL by this page.

components.functionality: Reads and interprets URL parameters (courseId, registrationStatus, optional registrationId) on initialization and on URL changes, orchestrates when getTrainingCourseDetails is called, and controls whether detailed course components, registration form, and confirmation panel are shown or disabled.

Requests detailed TrainingCourse data using getTrainingCourseDetails with the courseId provided by page-state-controller, manages loading and error states, and exposes the resulting TrainingCourse object to dependent display components.

Displays high-level information about the course, such as TrainingCourse.title, level, deliveryMethod, location, price, currency, and maxParticipants, in a concise summary section that is updated whenever TrainingCourse data changes.
Shows schedule-related attributes of the course using TrainingCourse.startDate, endDate, and durationDays, helping the user understand when and for how long the training will take place.
Presents the detailed course description (and any embedded syllabus or module breakdown) using TrainingCourse.description, updating whenever the TrainingCourse data is refreshed.

Provides input fields to capture registration details (fullName, email, company, phone, participantsCount) tied to the current courseId. Performs client-side validation, and on successful validation, calls submitTrainingRegistration to create a TrainingRegistration for the given course.

Displays confirmation details after a successful registration submission, using the TrainingRegistration object and message returned from submitTrainingRegistration when available, or a generic success state when registrationStatus=success is present in the URL but detailed registration data is not available.

Shows error or warning messages when courseId is missing, invalid, or when getTrainingCourseDetails fails, and can disable or visually indicate that the registration form is not available until a valid course is loaded.

Accepts a text query and triggers globalSearch with the entered query and a set of entityTypes covering machineModels, spareParts, documents, and caseStudies, then forwards the results to search-results-overlay for display.

Receives aggregated globalSearch results and displays them grouped by entity type (machineModels, spareParts, documents, caseStudies) on top of the Training Detail page. Allows the user to navigate to a selected result or close the overlay without affecting the current course and registration state.

layout.grid_system: Likely a centered fixed-width grid (approximately 1140–1200px max-width) on a full-bleed dark background, conceptually compatible with a 12-column grid with wide outer gutters.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the mockup’s linear, top‑to‑bottom flow inside a full-width dark shell with a centered content column (max-width ~1140–1200px). A fixed-height header (outside this spec) sits at the top. Immediately below it, the training-summary-panel assumes the hero role as the dominant first content block, with the training-schedule-info visually attached to it as a right-side info card. Beneath this hero region, sections stack vertically: first the training-description-block, then the training-registration-form. The registration-confirmation-panel and course-error-message appear conditionally in the same central column, either replacing or overlaying the primary blocks. The global-search-bar and search-results-overlay float above the main content, aligned to the top center, following the mockup’s pattern of utilities overlaid on the hero.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the mockup’s linear, top‑to‑bottom flow inside a full-width dark shell with a centered content column (max-width ~1140–1200px). A fixed-height header (outside this spec) sits at the top. Immediately below it, the training-summary-panel assumes the hero role as the dominant first content block, with the training-schedule-info visually attached to it as a right-side info card. Beneath this hero region, sections stack vertically: first the training-description-block, then the training-registration-form. The registration-confirmation-panel and course-error-message appear conditionally in the same central column, either replacing or overlaying the primary blocks. The global-search-bar and search-results-overlay float above the main content, aligned to the top center, following the mockup’s pattern of utilities overlaid on the hero.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The main content column is centered on a charcoal background, occupying roughly 70–80% of the viewport width on desktop, with 10–15% dark margin on either side. The header takes about 10–15% of viewport height; the training-summary-panel (hero) takes 50–70% of the remaining visible height on initial load. Vertical spacing between major sections (summary → description → form) follows a 48–64px rhythm, using the 8px-based scale. Interactive blocks (cards, form) have 16–24px internal padding. Overlay elements like the global-search-bar and search-results-overlay hug the top center but sit 16–24px below the header, leaving a consistent breathing space around them and the hero.
layout_strategies.content_density: Overall density is low and spacious. There is generous negative space around the hero and each subsequent block, emphasizing a premium, product-centric feel. Text sections (description, schedule) are set in narrow, readable columns, avoiding long line lengths. The registration form is visually substantial but uncluttered, with clear grouping and comfortable vertical spacing (16–24px) between fields. Overlays (search results, error messages, confirmation) are compact and focused, with minimal but clear supporting text.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow preserves the mockup’s F/Z pattern. The user’s eye is first drawn to the logo/nav in the header (top-left), then sweeps horizontally across the navigation and any search trigger. From there, the gaze drops diagonally into the center of the screen where the training-summary-panel dominates, then sweeps across to the attached training-schedule-info card on the right. Below the hero, the eye continues downward through the training-description-block into the training-registration-form, which acts as the primary action endpoint. Overlay utilities—the global-search-bar and search-results-overlay near the top center, and any ephemeral messaging—act as secondary attractors after the primary training content has been processed.

layout.overall_description: The Training Detail page sits inside a full-bleed charcoal background with a fixed industrial header above and a centered, max-width content column below. At first view, the page is almost entirely dominated by a cinematic hero-like training-summary-panel: a wide, dark card with a large machine or workshop visual and course essentials, floating visually in the center of the screen with generous margins on all sides. Clipped to its right edge is the training-schedule-info card, a narrower vertical block that feels like part of the hero, reinforcing dates and location details. Below this hero region, the layout collapses into clean, stacked sections: a wide training-description-block that reads like a technical overview, followed by a substantial training-registration-form card that stretches down the page in a single, clear column. Error or empty states replace the hero with a centered status message, while a successful registration swaps the form for a confirmation card occupying the same footprint. When invoked, a global-search-bar slides into focus near the top center over the hero, with a dark search-results-overlay panel hanging directly below it, dimming the rest of the page slightly but keeping the central column and industrial aesthetic intact.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page-state-controller is non-visual and occupies no explicit space in the layout. Conceptually, it sits at the top of the page’s component hierarchy, just below the global shell and header, orchestrating whether the main column displays the training-summary-panel + supporting sections, the course-error-message, or the registration-confirmation-panel. It does not render any visible frame, spacing, or background; instead, it controls which of the visible components appear in the central content column at any given time.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Purely logical/stateful; no rendered footprint. It affects spatial behavior of other components by toggling their visibility and replacing sections in the main content flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The training-detail-loader also has no standalone visual container; it wires into the same central content column that houses the hero and subsequent sections. While data is loading, it can inject skeleton placeholders or a subtle loading indicator into the space where the training-summary-panel and training-schedule-info would normally appear—centered within the max-width column, about 48px below the header. Any loader visuals should mimic the proportions of the hero block (occupying roughly 70–80% of the column width and 40–50% of viewport height) so the page structure feels stable as data arrives.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Logical/data-loading; when active it temporarily occupies the hero region with placeholders but otherwise yields its space to the actual content components.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The training-summary-panel functions as the hero section and sits at the top of the main content column, centered horizontally on the page and starting approximately 48px below the bottom edge of the header. It spans about 70–80% of the content column’s width, leaving equal dark margins on left and right, and occupies roughly 50–70% of the visible viewport height on desktop. The panel reads as a broad, cinematic card, potentially with a large CNC or workshop image as the main backdrop, with the training title and key metadata aligned toward the left half. There is generous internal padding (around 24px on all sides) so text and imagery breathe. The training-schedule-info component docks to the right side of this hero, so the summary’s right edge feels visually integrated rather than abruptly cut. On smaller screens, the panel collapses to full width with the schedule card stacking below, but maintains ample vertical spacing above and below to preserve the premium, low-density feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline within the main content flow, but visually treated as a hero. Its height is flexible based on content but constrained to feel like a large, dominant block on initial viewport load.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The training-schedule-info appears as a distinct, vertically oriented card visually attached to the right side of the training-summary-panel. On desktop, it occupies roughly 25–30% of the hero’s total width and aligns vertically with the hero’s top edge, leaving a small inset margin (~16px) from the outer right and bottom edges of the overall hero area. This makes it feel like a modular, snap-on information module that highlights date, time, duration, location, and remaining capacity. The card has a slightly elevated look against the dark background, with 16–24px internal padding and clear separation between each line item using 8–16px vertical spacing. As the viewport narrows (e.g., tablet or mobile), the schedule card breaks away from the right dock and stacks directly beneath the hero summary, still centered within the main column and maintaining about 32–48px vertical gap from the hero to keep the layout airy.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline and anchored to the training-summary-panel on large screens; responsive behavior stacks it below the hero summary on narrower viewports while preserving central alignment and section spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The training-description-block sits directly below the hero region (summary + schedule), forming the first full-width text section in the central column. It begins approximately 48–64px beneath the bottom edge of the hero, maintaining the generous section rhythm. The block spans roughly 70% of the content column width to keep line lengths readable, aligned flush with the left edge of the hero’s inner content rather than stretching all the way to the column’s extremes. This creates a subtle text column that feels nested under the hero. Internal padding of about 24px frames the text within a low-contrast dark card or simply a subtle tonal change from the background, with 16–24px vertical spacing between headings, paragraphs, and any bullet points. The overall block height grows with content but is visually separated from the registration form below by another 48–64px gap.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline section in the vertical stack, fully responsive width-wise and allowed to grow vertically with content while preserving consistent top and bottom margins.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The training-registration-form is positioned beneath the training-description-block as the primary action area of the page. It starts about 48–64px below the description, maintaining the spacious rhythm. The form is contained in a solid, dark, rectangular card centered in the content column, spanning about 60–70% of the column’s width on desktop. The left edge of the form aligns visually with the left edge of the description text to create a clear vertical spine. Inside the card, the form fields are arranged in a single vertical column for simplicity, with 16–24px vertical spacing between fields and 16–24px padding from the card edges. The main submit button sits toward the bottom-right of the form’s content area, aligning with the F/Z pattern’s lower horizontal stroke. The form is tall enough to feel substantial and may exceed the viewport height, enabling vertical scrolling while the header and hero remain above. On smaller devices, the form expands to near-full width with reduced horizontal padding but preserves generous vertical spacing to avoid crowding.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline card in the main flow; its height is content-driven and scrollable. It is replaced or visually overlaid by the registration-confirmation-panel when the registration completes successfully.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The registration-confirmation-panel occupies the same general region as the training-registration-form to keep context and reduce cognitive load. When shown after a successful submission, it appears centered in the main content column, starting where the form previously began. On desktop, it matches or slightly reduces the form’s width (around 50–60% of the content column) to create a more compact, celebratory card. There is ample internal padding (24–32px) with a concise success message, key registration details, and possibly a small icon or badge aligned near the top-left of the card. The confirmation content is vertically balanced with generous white space below any text and actions, so it feels open and calm rather than dense. The hero and description above remain visible if the user scrolls upward, but the confirmation card is the clear focal point in the lower part of the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline replacement for the registration form within the main flow; it either fully replaces the form component or overlays it within the same spatial footprint without changing the surrounding section spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The course-error-message appears when the training-detail-loader cannot retrieve valid course data and thus substitutes the hero region. In this state, instead of the training-summary-panel and training-schedule-info, a compact, centered status card appears in the main content column about 48px below the header. This card spans roughly 40–50% of the content column width, creating a tight, focused block surrounded by generous dark margins. The message and any retry actions are centered within the card with 24px internal padding and clear 16px spacing between lines. Because it stands alone in the center of the screen, it is visually striking despite its smaller footprint. In less severe cases (e.g., partial errors), it can also appear as a slim banner just above the training-summary-panel, full width of the main column, with around 16px height and 8–12px padding, but the primary state replaces the hero for fatal courseId errors.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally inline, occupying the hero’s space when critical errors occur. For minor errors, it can behave like a dismissible banner attached to the top of the main content column while leaving the underlying layout intact.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The global-search-bar is a floating utility that appears near the top center of the viewport, visually aligned with the main content column but not pushing content down. When activated (e.g., from a header icon), it materializes as a wide, low-profile search field spanning roughly 60% of the content column’s width, centered horizontally and positioned about 16–24px below the bottom of the header. It sits above the training-summary-panel, overlaying the top portion of the hero without obscuring it entirely, thanks to its slim height and dark, semi-elevated background. There is 12–16px internal padding left and right, with the search icon on the left and text input area extending toward the right. The surrounding background may subtly dim or blur, but the main content remains visible beneath, keeping the sense of depth while emphasizing the search utility.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Overlaid and fixed near the top of the viewport when open, independent of scrolling. It does not reflow the underlying content but hovers above it, respecting the centered grid and overall margins.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The search-results-overlay is directly associated with the global-search-bar, appearing as a dark, semi-elevated panel attached to its bottom edge. It matches the search bar’s width (approximately 60% of the content column) and is centered horizontally in the same position. The panel drops down from the bar, extending downward across the hero area and, if necessary, beyond the fold, with a maximum height of about 50–60% of the viewport before becoming internally scrollable. Results are listed in a vertical stack with generous 12–16px padding between items and 16–24px padding around the panel’s edges. The rest of the page behind the overlay is subtly darkened to keep focus on search, but the hero’s shape and layout remain faintly visible, maintaining a sense of continuity with the underlying page. The overlay ends well above the bottom edge of the viewport, leaving at least 24–32px margin to avoid feeling cramped.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Floating overlay anchored to and positioned directly below the global-search-bar, fixed relative to the viewport while open. It sits on top of all main content and scrolls internally for longer result lists, while the background page scroll is typically locked or deemphasized.

name: Dealer Locator
title: Dealer Locator
description: Page for searching and filtering nearby dealers and authorized service centers by location, radius, and dealer type, with results shown as a list and optionally on a map.

core_features: Accept a postal/ZIP code and radius, trigger the searchDealers interface, and display nearby dealers with their distance from the search location.
Provide filters for dealer attributes, including dealer type and an 'authorized service center' filter synchronized with the isAuthorizedServiceCenter URL parameter.
Provide sorting options (e.g., by distance or other supported attributes) synchronized with the sortBy URL parameter and passed through to searchDealers.
Display search results in a structured list and optionally on a map, with each result showing key dealer information and distance.
Allow navigation from each dealer result to a dealer detail page via dealer_detail.html?dealerId=...
Initialize the page state (forms, filters, results) from incoming URL parameters (postalCode, radiusKm, isAuthorizedServiceCenter, sortBy), and update the URL when the user changes search criteria.
Expose the site-wide global search bar using the globalSearch interface for searching across machines, spare parts, documents, and case studies.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Arrive with pre-filled location from another page
1. User clicks a 'Find a Dealer' link on index.html or contact.html that navigates to dealer_locator.html with postalCode and radiusKm (and optionally isAuthorizedServiceCenter) in the URL.
2. On page load, the URL-state component reads postalCode, radiusKm, isAuthorizedServiceCenter, and sortBy from the URL.
3. The dealer search form is pre-populated with the postalCode and radiusKm from the URL; the 'authorized service center' toggle reflects isAuthorizedServiceCenter.
4. The page invokes searchDealers using the URL-derived values and an initial filters object (including isAuthorizedServiceCenter and any default or URL-based dealer type filters), and passes sortBy if present.
5. The dealer results list and map panel render the returned dealers with distances.
6. User optionally adjusts radius, filters, or sorting; each change updates the URL parameters, re-invokes searchDealers, and refreshes the list and map.
7. User clicks a dealer entry, navigating to dealer_detail.html?dealerId=<selectedDealerId>.

Workflow: Start a new search directly on the Dealer Locator page
1. User navigates to dealer_locator.html without URL parameters.
2. The URL-state component detects missing postalCode and radiusKm and leaves the search form empty (or with implementation-defined defaults) and does not call searchDealers until the user submits.
3. User enters a postal/ZIP code and a radius, optionally selects a dealer type and toggles the 'authorized service center' filter.
4. User submits the search form; the form component updates the URL with postalCode, radiusKm, isAuthorizedServiceCenter, selected dealer type (e.g., dealerType), and a sortBy value (if selected), then invokes searchDealers with these values.
5. The dealer results list and map panel render the returned dealers with distances.
6. User changes sorting (e.g., to another supported attribute); the sort control updates the sortBy URL parameter, re-calls searchDealers with the new sortBy, and updates the results.

Workflow: Use global search from the Dealer Locator page
1. User types a query in the site-wide global search bar at the top of the page.
2. On submit, the global search component calls globalSearch with the query and a configured entityTypes list.
3. Based on the implementation, the site either displays search results in a global search results UI or navigates to a dedicated search results page, without altering the dealer locator’s dealer search state.

Workflow: Refine to authorized service centers only
1. User has already performed a dealer search and sees results.
2. User activates the 'Authorized service centers only' toggle in the filter bar.
3. The filter component updates the isAuthorizedServiceCenter URL parameter (for example, setting it to 'true') and updates any internal filter state.
4. The filter component triggers searchDealers with the updated filters object (including isAuthorizedServiceCenter = true) while keeping postalCode, radiusKm, and sortBy unchanged.
5. The results list and map refresh to show only dealers matching the authorized service center filter.

Workflow: Click from map marker to dealer detail
1. User performs a search, and the map panel displays dealer markers based on the current searchDealers result set.
2. User clicks a marker on the map.
3. The map component identifies the corresponding dealer in the current results and highlights the matching row in the results list.
4. If the user clicks a 'View details' action from the marker or list, the page navigates to dealer_detail.html?dealerId=<selectedDealerId>.
interactions: Entering or editing the postal/ZIP code input updates the internal form state but does not trigger searchDealers until the user submits or an explicit 'Search' action is taken.
Changing the radius input updates the internal form state and URL (when submitted) and influences the radiusKm argument passed to searchDealers.
Toggling the 'Authorized service center' filter updates both the filter state and isAuthorizedServiceCenter URL parameter, then re-invokes searchDealers using the updated filters object.
Selecting a dealer type filter (e.g., dealer, service_center, sales_and_service) updates an internal filter state and, if implemented, a dealerType URL parameter, then re-calls searchDealers.

Changing a sort option (e.g., distance ascending or other backend-supported sort codes) updates the sortBy URL parameter and triggers a new searchDealers call using the same postalCode, radiusKm, and filters.
Submitting the dealer search form updates the URL parameters (postalCode, radiusKm, isAuthorizedServiceCenter, dealerType if used, sortBy if selected) and calls searchDealers with those values.
Clicking on a dealer row in the results list triggers navigation to dealer_detail.html with dealerId set to the selected dealer’s id.
Clicking a dealer marker on the map selects the corresponding dealer in the results list (e.g., scrolls to and highlights it) without changing URL parameters, keeping search state intact.
Using the global site search bar triggers the globalSearch interface with the query and an entityTypes list, without modifying dealer_locator.html URL parameters.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The dealer locator page’s functional state is derived from and synchronized with URL parameters so that the search is shareable and bookmarkable.

Primary URL parameters (incoming and maintained):
- postalCode (string): When present, the page treats this as the center point for dealer search. On initial load, if both postalCode and radiusKm are available, the page immediately calls searchDealers with these values.
- radiusKm (number): The numeric search radius. On load, if present with postalCode, it populates the radius field and is passed directly to searchDealers.
- isAuthorizedServiceCenter (string): When set (e.g., 'true'), the filter bar’s 'Authorized service center' toggle is activated. The page maps this string to a boolean (true when the value represents an active state) and passes it into the filters arguments of searchDealers.
- sortBy (string): Represents the active sorting mode (for example, a backend-defined code such as distance-based sorting). If present, it initializes the sort control. If absent, the implementation may rely on the backend’s default sort behavior while the UI indicates that default.

Additional state-related parameters (optional, defined by this page’s logic):
- dealerType (string, optional): Encodes the selected dealer type filter. Its value, when present, is mapped onto the Dealer.dealerType enum and included in the filters object sent to searchDealers. When omitted, no dealerType filter is applied.

State flow:
1. On initial page load, a URL-state manager parses the current URL for postalCode, radiusKm, isAuthorizedServiceCenter, sortBy, and dealerType. These values initialize the search form, filter bar, and sort control.
2. If postalCode and radiusKm are both present, the page automatically calls searchDealers with:
   - postalCode (from URL),
   - radiusKm (from URL),
   - filters object containing: isAuthorizedServiceCenter mapped from the URL value, and dealerType derived from the dealerType URL parameter when provided,
   - sortBy (from URL, if present).
3. Whenever the user submits the search form or changes filters/sorting, the page constructs a new URL with the current postalCode, radiusKm, isAuthorizedServiceCenter (if relevant), dealerType (if used), and sortBy. It then updates the browser URL (e.g., via history or location APIs) and calls searchDealers again with the matching values.
4. The page listens for URL changes (e.g., browser back/forward). On URL change, it re-parses the parameters, updates the UI controls (search form, filter bar, sort selection), and if sufficient parameters are available (at least postalCode and radiusKm), re-executes searchDealers. This ensures that navigation history fully restores prior search states.
5. The results list and map do not maintain independent persistent state beyond what is derived from the latest searchDealers response; they re-render entirely based on each new response. Any selection (e.g., highlighted dealer) is a transient view state and is not encoded into URL parameters unless explicitly required by the implementation.

components.functionality: Parses and manages URL parameters (postalCode, radiusKm, isAuthorizedServiceCenter, sortBy, and optional dealerType), initializes form and filter state from them on load, and updates them when the user changes search criteria. Triggers searchDealers whenever sufficient search inputs are available and URL-based state changes.

Provides inputs for postal/ZIP code and radius. Reads initial values from the URL-state manager, lets the user edit them, and on submit updates the URL parameters and invokes searchDealers with the new postalCode and radiusKm plus existing filters and sortBy.

Displays and manages dealer-specific filters, including an 'Authorized service center' toggle and dealer type selection. Synchronizes the 'Authorized service center' toggle with the isAuthorizedServiceCenter URL parameter. When filters change, it updates relevant URL parameters (isAuthorizedServiceCenter and dealerType if implemented) and requests a new dealer search via searchDealers.

Allows the user to select a sort mode for dealer results (for example, by distance or other backend-supported attributes). Initializes its value from the sortBy URL parameter. When the sort option changes, it updates sortBy in the URL and triggers searchDealers with the same postalCode, radiusKm, and filters but the new sortBy value.

Displays the list of dealers returned by searchDealers. For each dealer, shows the core Dealer fields and the associated distanceKm. Supports row selection and navigation to dealer_detail.html when a dealer is clicked.

Represents a single dealer entry within the results list. Binds to one dealer object and its distanceKm, and exposes a click action to navigate to the dealer’s detail page using dealer_detail.html?dealerId=<dealer.id>.

Optionally renders dealers returned by searchDealers as markers on a map, using each dealer’s latitude and longitude when available. Keeps markers synchronized with the current results list and allows selecting a dealer by clicking a marker. Marker selection highlights the corresponding dealer in the results list and may provide a direct navigation control to dealer_detail.html.

Indicates the current state of the dealer search, such as loading, empty results, or error states, based on the status of the latest searchDealers call. It reacts to search invocations and responses to give feedback without storing independent business data.

Site-wide search component available in the header on this page. Accepts a text query, invokes the globalSearch interface with that query and an entityTypes list, and then either displays global results or navigates to a central search results page depending on site-wide behavior. It does not modify dealer locator URL parameters.

layout.grid_system: Likely a centered fixed-width grid (approximately 1140–1200px max-width) on a full-bleed dark background, conceptually compatible with a 12-column grid with wide outer gutters.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the same top-to-bottom stacking as the mockup: a fixed-height dark header at the very top (logo, primary navigation, global site search, language switcher), then a centered main content column on a full-bleed charcoal background. Inside the main column, the dealer locator content is grouped into a single, hero-like section: dealer-search-form at the top, followed by the dealer-filter-bar, then a split layout where the dealer-results-list (with status indicator and sort control) sits in a left column and the dealer-map-panel occupies a larger right column. All components are aligned to an invisible 12-column grid within a max-width content container (~1140–1200px) centered on the page.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the same top-to-bottom stacking as the mockup: a fixed-height dark header at the very top (logo, primary navigation, global site search, language switcher), then a centered main content column on a full-bleed charcoal background. Inside the main column, the dealer locator content is grouped into a single, hero-like section: dealer-search-form at the top, followed by the dealer-filter-bar, then a split layout where the dealer-results-list (with status indicator and sort control) sits in a left column and the dealer-map-panel occupies a larger right column. All components are aligned to an invisible 12-column grid within a max-width content container (~1140–1200px) centered on the page.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header occupies roughly 10–15% of the viewport height. Below it, the dealer locator section fills most of the remaining vertical space, echoing the hero image dominance from the mockup. The main content column uses about 70–80% of the viewport width, leaving 10–15% dark margin on each side. Within that main column, the search form and filter bar are full-width, while the map/list area is split roughly 40% width for the list (left) and 60% width for the map (right). Vertical spacing between stacked elements follows the 48–64px scale for major breaks and 16–24px for related controls, preserving a spacious feel.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content stays low-density and typography-led. Each functional band (search form, filters, list + map) has ample breathing room above and below. Inputs, chips, and controls use 12–16px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding, with 8px between icons and labels. The list items are not tightly packed; they read as individual cards with clear separation. The map takes up a large continuous block of space, mirroring the single cinematic hero image of the mockup, while surrounding dark negative space keeps the interface calm and premium.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow reuses the F/Z pattern. The eye hits the bright yellow logo in the top-left header, travels across the navigation and global search toward the language selector on the right, then drops diagonally down to the center of the page where the dealer-search-form sits. From there it moves down into the left edge of the map/list section (status indicator and list heading), then travels horizontally across to the more visually dominant map on the right. The main interaction focus is therefore the central search-and-map block, with the results list providing secondary scanning down the left column. Controls like sort and filters act as smaller, secondary anchors along this path.

layout.overall_description: The Dealer Locator page sits inside a full-bleed charcoal shell, with a fixed-height dark header band at the top and a centered main content column beneath. The header echoes the industrial brand: a saturated yellow stencil-style logo at the far left, a clean row of navigation links to its right, a compact global search bar further right, and a language selector anchored at the far right edge. Below, after a generous 48–64px gap, the main dealer locator section appears as a large, hero-like block floating within the dark background. At the top of this block, a wide dealer search form is centered and slightly inset from the sides, giving the sense of a control console above the machine. Immediately below runs a slim, full-width filter bar for toggling dealer types and radius. Together, these two bands set up the search intent. Underneath, the layout splits into two columns: on the left, a vertically scrollable list of dealers in a dark, subtle card container; on the right, a taller, more expansive map panel that visually dominates the section, echoing the cinematic hero image from the mockup. A compact status line at the top-left of the list and a sort dropdown on the top-right of the list column form a thin horizontal control strip, aligning with the top of the map. The entire map/list block is inset from the viewport edges, with 40–64px of dark margin around it, creating a focused, premium, instrument-panel feeling consistent with an industrial CNC brand.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This is a non-visual infrastructure component. It is conceptually anchored to the overall page, not to any specific visible element. It does not occupy any visible space in the layout and does not affect the spatial arrangement of other components. Instead, it sits logically behind the dealer locator section (search form, filters, list, map), managing URL parameters corresponding to those visible controls.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendered utility; no box in the visual layout. It behaves globally for the page, synchronizing state with URL regardless of scroll position or viewport size.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dealer search form is the first visible component in the main content area, positioned directly beneath the global header. It is centered horizontally within the max-width content container, starting approximately 48–64px below the bottom edge of the header. The form spans about 60–70% of the content width, leaving equal dark margins on both sides within the container, reinforcing the focused, console-like feel. Vertically, the form is compact but comfortable: a single row or two stacked rows of inputs (e.g., location, radius) with 12–16px vertical padding inside the form and 16–24px horizontal padding on each side. There is roughly 24px of vertical space below the form before the filter bar, maintaining the spacious rhythm seen in the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the main column on desktop; it scrolls away with the content. On smaller screens, it can expand to nearly 100% of the screen width inside the same centered column, with the same generous top margin below the header.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dealer filter bar sits directly below the dealer search form, aligned to the same left and right edges as the form but spanning slightly wider—up to 80–90% of the content width—to read as a full-width control strip for the locator. It is a low-profile horizontal band, about 48px tall, with filter chips, toggles, or dropdowns laid out from left to right. The left edge of the filter bar lines up with the left edge of the future list column, visually tying it to the results. There is approximately 16–24px of vertical gap between the bottom of this bar and the top of the map/list section, preserving the hierarchy between input controls and results.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element that scrolls with the page. On desktop, it remains a single row; on smaller screens, controls can wrap to two lines within the same band height expanded to ~72–96px.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The sort control is positioned at the top-right corner of the left results column, just below the filter bar and aligned horizontally with the status indicator on the left. It sits inside a slim horizontal strip at the top of the results list area, about 40–56px tall. The sort dropdown (or segmented control) is right-aligned, with its right edge lining up vertically with the right edge of the results list column. There is a 8–16px gap between the sort control and the top boundary of the list items, clearly separating list metadata from the first dealer entry.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the results list area. As the user scrolls the list (if the list has its own scroll), the sort control remains anchored at the top of that list area; if the entire page scrolls, it scrolls away with the content. On small screens where the layout becomes single-column, it shifts to the top-right of the list, still just below filters.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dealer results list occupies the left column of the main results area, directly beneath the filter bar. The overall results section is a rectangular panel centered in the page; within this, the list column is about 38–42% of the panel’s width, leaving 58–62% on the right for the map. The top of the list aligns precisely with the top of the map panel, giving a single, crisp horizontal line. The list container has subtle padding (16–24px) on all sides and a slight elevation or tonal difference from the background to read as a dark card stacked in front of the shell. Vertically, the list stretches down to near the bottom of the viewport, leaving 40–64px of dark margin at the bottom, and can scroll internally if there are many results. Inside, dealer-result-items are stacked in a single vertical column with consistent spacing between them.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: On desktop, the list sits in a fixed-width left column next to the map. It can have its own vertical scroll while the map remains fully visible and possibly sticky. On narrower viewports, the layout can collapse to place the list above the map, both spanning close to 100% of the content width, maintaining vertical stacking consistency.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each dealer result item appears as a low-profile dark card within the results list column. It spans nearly the full width of the list container, with 16–20px of internal padding on all sides. Items are separated by 8–16px of vertical space to keep them visually distinct yet compact. Text content is arranged in a vertical hierarchy: dealer name at the top, address and contact details beneath, and any tags (e.g., 'Service Center', 'Authorized Dealer') aligned to the right or bottom in smaller type, all respecting the minimal, typographic style. The cards maintain a flat, modern look with no heavy borders; separation is achieved through spacing and slight tonal contrast against the list background.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static children of the results list. They flow top-to-bottom and reflow to full-width cards in a single-column layout on smaller screens. When the list scrolls, these items scroll naturally within it.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The map panel is the dominant visual element in the main content area, acting as the functional equivalent of the cinematic hero image from the mockup. It is positioned to the right of the results list, sharing the same top edge directly beneath the filter bar. The panel occupies roughly 58–62% of the width of the results block and runs nearly the full height of that block, creating a tall, expansive visual field. Its left edge aligns with the right edge of the results list, with a narrow 24–32px gap between them, preserving generous whitespace while clearly linking them. The panel itself spans almost the full height available between the filter bar above and the lower margin below, with subtle rounded corners and no heavy border, making the live map content the focal point. The map’s right edge maintains an inset of about 40–64px from the viewport edge, matching the hero image framing from the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: On desktop, the map panel can be sticky relative to the viewport: as the user scrolls the page, the map remains visible while the results list can scroll independently within its column. On smaller screens, the map drops below the results list and becomes a full-width block (within the same centered column), losing the two-column sticky behavior but keeping a strong visual presence.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The search status indicator appears as a compact text or pill-shaped element at the top-left of the results list area, just under the filter bar. It sits on the same horizontal line as the sort control (which is on the right), forming a thin bar of list metadata above the first dealer item. Its left edge aligns with the left padding of the results list container, and there is about 8–12px of vertical space below the indicator before the first result. The indicator is subtle—small type, perhaps with a muted highlight color—so it is readable without competing visually with the map or the main search form.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element inside the results list header row. It scrolls with the list and remains anchored just above the list items. On smaller screens, it remains aligned to the left edge above the list, with the sort control stacking to its right or below depending on available width.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The global search bar is integrated into the top site header, in line with the logo and navigation. The header runs full-width across the top of the viewport with a fixed height (roughly 72–88px) and is centered around the primary content column. The logo sits at the far left, navigation items follow toward the center, and the global search bar is placed to the right of the main navigation, occupying about 240–320px of width. It is horizontally centered along the header’s baseline, with 16–24px of horizontal padding inside and a low-profile dark input field that matches the industrial aesthetic. To its right, aligned with the right edge of the centered grid, is the language selector. The global search bar has adequate clearance above and below (8–16px internal margin within the header) so that the header feels dense but not crowded.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed within the header. If the header is implemented as sticky, the global search bar stays pinned to the top as the user scrolls down into the dealer locator content. On smaller screens, it can collapse into an icon that expands over the navigation, but still remains part of the header region, above the dealer-specific search form.

name: Dealer Detail
title: Dealer Detail
description: Displays detailed information and contact options for a specific dealer or service center selected from the dealer locator.

core_features: Read the dealerId from the URL and load detailed dealer information via getDealerDetails.
Display dealer address, contact details, website, dealer type, service capabilities, opening hours, and coverage regions based on the Dealer entity.
Provide a dealer-specific contact form that sends messages using sendDealerMessage with the current dealerId.
Show success or error feedback after message submission without leaving the page.
Allow users to perform a global search across machines, spare parts, documents, and case studies using globalSearch.
user_workflows:
View dealer details:
1. User navigates from dealer_locator.html to dealer_detail.html with a dealerId URL parameter.
2. On page load, the page reads dealerId from the URL.
3. The page calls getDealerDetails(dealerId).
4. When data is returned, the dealer information panel displays the dealer's name, address, contact details, website, dealer type, service capabilities, opening hours, and coverage regions.
5. If getDealerDetails fails or returns no valid dealer, an error state is shown instead of the details.

Send a message to the dealer:
1. User views the dealer detail page with dealerId present in the URL.
2. The contact form is pre-associated with the current dealerId.
3. User enters their name, email, and message into the form fields.
4. User submits the form.
5. The page validates required fields locally (e.g., name not empty, email format, message not empty).
6. If validation passes, the page calls sendDealerMessage with dealerId, name, email, and message.
7. When the API response is received, the page checks the success flag.
8. If success is true, the page displays a confirmation message using the feedback and/or DealerMessage data and may clear or disable the form.
9. If success is false, an error message is displayed, and the user can modify the form and resubmit.

Use global search from the dealer detail page:
1. User focuses the global search input in the site header (available on this page).
2. User types a search query and submits it.
3. The page calls globalSearch with the query and an entityTypes configuration appropriate for the global search (e.g., including machines, spare parts, documents, and case studies).
4. When results are returned, the search results panel on this page shows grouped results for MachineModel, SparePart, DownloadableDocument, and CaseStudy entities.
5. User selects a result, which navigates them to the corresponding detail page using that entity's URL pattern (handled outside this page’s core specification).
interactions: URL-based dealer selection: Changing the dealerId in the URL (e.g., via navigation from dealer_locator.html) triggers a new getDealerDetails call and updates all dealer-related components.
Contact form input: User types into name, email, and message fields; input changes update local component state but do not call any interfaces until form submission.
Contact form submission: User clicks the submit button; the form validates inputs and then calls sendDealerMessage. The response updates the confirmation/error component and may reset the form.
Global search input: User types a query and either presses Enter or clicks a search button; this triggers globalSearch and updates the search results panel.
Retry on failed message: If sendDealerMessage returns success = false, the user can adjust the form inputs and submit again, triggering another sendDealerMessage call with the same dealerId.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary state driver is the dealerId URL parameter (dealer_detail.html?dealerId=...). On initial load and whenever dealerId in the URL changes, the page calls getDealerDetails(dealerId) to fetch fresh Dealer data. If dealerId is missing or invalid, the page does not call getDealerDetails and instead presents an error or 'dealer not found' state, while still allowing global search. The dealerId is also passed to sendDealerMessage on contact form submission to associate each DealerMessage with the correct dealer. The URL does not change during or after message submission; message status (success or error) is maintained as in-memory page state only. Global search does not alter the URL in this page specification; it updates an in-page results component based on globalSearch responses.

components.functionality: Reads the dealerId parameter from the current URL and exposes it to other components. Observes URL changes (such as browser navigation) and updates the dealerId state, which in turn triggers re-fetching of dealer details.
event_handlers: onInit: Parse dealerId from the URL query string and store it in page-level state.
onUrlChange: Re-parse dealerId from the updated URL and update page-level state, triggering dependent components to refresh.

components.functionality: Fetches detailed information for the currently selected dealer using getDealerDetails and manages loading/error states.
event_handlers: onDealerIdChange: When dealerId from url-state-manager is set or changed, call getDealerDetails(dealerId).
onLoadStart: Set loading state to true and clear previous error state.
onLoadSuccess: Store the returned Dealer entity in shared state and set loading state to false.
onLoadError: Store an error state (e.g., generic fetch error or 'dealer not found') and set loading state to false.

components.functionality: Displays core dealer information such as name, address, contact details, website, and dealer type based on the Dealer entity loaded by dealer-detail-fetcher.
event_handlers: onDealerDataChange: Re-render dealer name, full address (addressLine1, addressLine2, city, stateRegion, postalCode, country), phone, email, website, dealerType, and isAuthorizedServiceCenter indicator whenever Dealer data changes.
onErrorStateChange: If dealer-detail-fetcher sets an error state, hide dealer info and display an appropriate error message instead.

components.functionality: Shows the dealer's serviceCapabilities as a list to indicate what services (such as sales, service, or training) are available at this location.
event_handlers: onDealerDataChange: Read the serviceCapabilities array from Dealer and render it as a list or tag set whenever Dealer data changes.
onEmptyCapabilities: If serviceCapabilities is absent or empty, hide this section or show a generic 'no capabilities specified' message.

components.functionality: Displays the dealer's openingHours information, if provided, in a readable format.
event_handlers: onDealerDataChange: Read openingHours from Dealer and render it; if openingHours is not provided, hide this component or show a 'not provided' state.

components.functionality: Displays the coverageRegions from the Dealer entity so users can see which geographic regions this dealer serves.
event_handlers: onDealerDataChange: Read coverageRegions array from Dealer and render each region as a list item when Dealer data updates.
onEmptyCoverage: If coverageRegions is absent or empty, hide this list or show a generic indication that coverage information is not specified.

components.functionality: Provides a contact form scoped to the current dealer, allowing users to send a message. On submit, it calls sendDealerMessage with dealerId, name, email, and message, and forwards the result to the status component.
event_handlers: onInit: Initialize local form state (empty name, email, message, no validation errors).
onInputChange: Update local form state for name, email, and message as the user types.
onValidate: Check that name, email, and message are non-empty and that email has a valid format; set validation error messages if invalid.
onSubmit: Prevent default form submission, run onValidate, and if valid call sendDealerMessage with the current dealerId from url-state-manager and form field values.
onSubmitStart: Disable the submit button and set a 'sending' state while awaiting API response.

onSubmitSuccess: Receive { success, dealerMessage, feedback } from sendDealerMessage. If success is true, update shared DealerMessage state (for this submission), clear or lock the form fields, reset 'sending' state, and trigger dealer-message-status to display confirmation.
onSubmitError: If sendDealerMessage fails or returns success = false, set an error state and re-enable the submit button so the user can retry.

components.functionality: Displays feedback about the result of sendDealerMessage submissions, including success confirmations and error messages.
event_handlers: onMessageResponse: When dealer-contact-form receives a response from sendDealerMessage, read the success flag and feedback string and display an appropriate success or error message.
onNewSubmission: Clear previous status messages when the user starts editing the form again or begins a new submission.
onDealerChange: When dealerId changes (user navigates to another dealer via URL), clear any existing status messages and associated DealerMessage data.

components.functionality: Provides a global search input (typically in the site header) that allows users to search across machines, spare parts, documents, and case studies from this page using the globalSearch interface.
event_handlers: onInputChange: Update the local search query state as the user types.
onSubmit: When the user submits the search, call globalSearch with the current query and a configured entityTypes list to retrieve results across the supported entities.
onSearchStart: Set a searching state and optionally clear previous results while waiting for new ones.
onSearchSuccess: Store the returned machineModels, spareParts, documents, and caseStudies in shared search result state.
onSearchError: Display a search error message and keep or clear previous results based on design decisions.

components.functionality: Displays search results returned by globalSearch, grouped by entity type (MachineModel, SparePart, DownloadableDocument, CaseStudy), with navigation actions for each result.
event_handlers: onResultsChange: When global-search-form updates the search result state, render result lists for each entity type.

onResultClick: When a user clicks a result, navigate to the appropriate detail page by constructing a URL with that entity's identifier as a URL parameter (navigation targets are outside this page's core scope).
onClearResults: Provide an interaction (e.g., a clear button) that clears the displayed results without affecting other page state.

components.functionality: Manages and shows overall loading and error states for the dealer details area while getDealerDetails is in progress or has failed.
event_handlers: onLoadStart: Display a loading indicator for the dealer details area when dealer-detail-fetcher begins a request.
onLoadSuccess: Hide the loading indicator when Dealer data is successfully loaded so that dealer-info-panel and related components are visible.
onLoadError: Hide any loading indicator and show an error state if dealer-detail-fetcher reports an error (e.g., invalid dealerId or network error).

layout.grid_system: Likely a centered fixed-width grid (approximately 1140–1200px max-width) on a full-bleed dark background, conceptually compatible with a 12-column grid with wide outer gutters.
layout.layout_pattern: Maintain the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom structure: a fixed-height dark header with logo and navigation at the very top, followed by a single, dominant hero-like dealer info block centered in the content column. All other dealer details (services, hours, coverage, contact) stack vertically beneath this hero, organized into a loose two-column layout within the same centered grid. Utility elements (global search, status messages) overlay or float above this main flow, without disturbing the primary hero + stacked sections pattern.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Maintain the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom structure: a fixed-height dark header with logo and navigation at the very top, followed by a single, dominant hero-like dealer info block centered in the content column. All other dealer details (services, hours, coverage, contact) stack vertically beneath this hero, organized into a loose two-column layout within the same centered grid. Utility elements (global search, status messages) overlay or float above this main flow, without disturbing the primary hero + stacked sections pattern.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Use a full-bleed charcoal background with a centered content container ~1140–1200px wide. The header occupies roughly 10–15% of viewport height. Immediately below, the dealer info hero panel fills most of the remaining first viewport (about 60–70% of viewport height on desktop), inset with generous side margins so it occupies about 70–80% of the content width. Subsequent sections keep strong vertical separation: 48–64px gaps between major blocks, 24–32px within related groups. A floating bottom-right status element occupies minimal footprint, offset ~24–32px from the edges.
layout_strategies.content_density: Keep density low and premium: large dark areas surround each section, with generous padding inside cards and panels (16–24px horizontal, 12–16px vertical). Each section focuses on one main idea: a clean summary hero, then separate blocks for capabilities, hours, coverage, and contact. Lists remain short and well spaced, and long forms or lists scroll vertically rather than crowding the visible fold.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Follow the F/Z-pattern: the eye starts at the bright yellow logo in the header’s top-left, travels across navigation and the global search utility to the right, then drops diagonally into the centered dealer hero panel. Inside the main area, the eye moves across the hero, then continues down into the left column (services and hours) and across to the right column (coverage and contact). The floating bottom-right dealer message status chip/button acts as the final attractor at the end of this path, surfacing feedback without competing with the hero content.

layout.overall_description: The Dealer Detail page sits in a dark, full-bleed shell with a fixed-height charcoal header at the top: the industrial yellow stencil logo anchors the top-left, navigation links stretch horizontally beside it, and a compact global search trigger sits toward the top-right near the language control. Below the header, the eye lands on a large, cinematic dealer hero panel centered in the content column, occupying roughly two-thirds of the viewport height and about 75% of the content width, framed by generous dark margins on all sides. This hero panel presents core dealer information in a clean, typography-led layout, possibly set over a subtle photo or map treatment that echoes the brand’s mechanical aesthetic. Beneath this hero, the page transitions into a structured two-column zone: on the left, service capabilities and opening hours stack with clear section headings; on the right, coverage regions and a streamlined contact form mirror that stack, all within the same 12-column-based grid. Each section has ample vertical breathing room, separated by 48–64px gaps, with flat, uncluttered surfaces and restrained use of color. When search is activated, a dark, centered search panel drops down just under the header, overlaying the top of the hero; its results appear in a contained panel below it, still aligned to the central grid. Loading and error states temporarily replace the hero content with focused status cards, keeping the layout intact. Finally, a small, floating dealer message status element sits in the bottom-right corner of the viewport, offset from the edges, quietly signaling message success or issues without disrupting the main visual hierarchy.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The URL state manager operates invisibly and does not occupy any visible space in the layout. It conceptually sits behind the main content column, controlling which dealer data populates the hero and subsequent sections; the visual layout remains unaffected in size or position when state changes occur, aside from the data-driven components updating their content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, logically full-page in scope but with no rendered footprint; it influences which content is shown but does not alter the spatial arrangement.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dealer detail fetcher is also non-visual, but its effects are localized to the main content area directly beneath the header. While data is loading, it hands control to the page-level loading state, which occupies the same region where the dealer hero panel normally appears—centered, with roughly 70–80% of the content width and generous top margin under the header. Once data is loaded, it relinquishes that space to the dealer-info-panel and the stacked detail sections below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual by itself; it coordinates the appearance of the loading/error indicators within the hero area without changing the underlying grid or layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dealer info panel forms the primary hero section immediately below the header. It is centered within the fixed-width content column, occupying about 70–80% of the content width (roughly 8–9 of 12 grid columns) and about 60–70% of the viewport height on desktop. There is a 48–64px vertical gap between the bottom of the header and the top of this panel, and matching dark margins of 10–15% of the page width on the left and right, reinforcing the hero’s framed feel. Inside the panel, content is generally left-aligned: dealer name and key identifiers at the top-left, with secondary information (address, primary contact, machine brands, possibly a subtle map or machine image) arranged horizontally or in a two-column split within the hero. The panel’s top edge visually lines up with the start of the content column, and its bottom edge creates a clear horizontal baseline from which all subsequent sections descend.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, part of the normal document flow; it anchors the main content area and resizes responsively within the centered grid, maintaining generous padding (24–32px) inside the panel.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dealer service capabilities list appears directly beneath the dealer info hero, aligned to the left side of the main content grid. There is a 48–64px vertical gap between the bottom of the hero panel and the section heading for service capabilities. The section spans approximately the left 6–7 columns of the 12-column grid (about 55–60% of the content width), leaving the right column free for coverage regions and the contact form. Items in the list are stacked vertically with 8–16px spacing, and the entire section has 24–32px of internal padding top and bottom, maintaining the low-density, premium feel. Its top baseline aligns horizontally with the top of the dealer-coverage-regions section on the right, forming a balanced two-column row under the hero.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static and grid-bound; it participates in a two-column layout under the hero, collapsing to full-width stacked sections (service above coverage) on narrower screens while preserving vertical spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dealer opening hours section is positioned immediately below the service capabilities on the left side of the main content area, maintaining the same left alignment and column width. It starts after a 24–32px vertical gap beneath the capabilities section, creating a visually grouped left column of operational information. This section occupies roughly the same 6–7 grid columns as the capabilities list, with consistent padding and typography. The bottom of the opening hours block roughly aligns with or slightly exceeds the bottom of the contact form on the right, allowing the page to end in a visually coherent horizontal rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the left column; as the page narrows, it stacks below the service capabilities while keeping 24–32px of vertical separation and full-width alignment.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coverage regions section occupies the right side of the first content row below the hero, horizontally aligned with the top of the dealer-service-capabilities section. It spans about 5–6 of the 12 grid columns (approximately 40–45% of the content width), leaving a subtle central gutter between the left and right columns to keep the layout airy. The section sits 48–64px below the hero’s bottom edge, mirroring the vertical offset of the service capabilities, and uses a similar 24–32px internal padding with vertically stacked region items or tags spaced by 8–16px. This makes the left and right sections read as a coordinated pair of information blocks beneath the hero.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static in the right column; on smaller screens it collapses beneath the left column sections, becoming a full-width block with preserved vertical gaps.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dealer contact form is placed directly beneath the coverage regions section in the right column of the main content area. It begins after a 24–32px vertical gap under the coverage regions heading/content, maintaining the same right column width (5–6 grid columns, about 40–45% of the content width). The form fields are stacked vertically with 16–24px spacing, and the entire form is wrapped in a subtle panel or delineated block with generous internal padding (24–32px) to keep it readable and uncluttered on the dark background. The form’s top is roughly level with the mid-point of the opening hours section on the left, and its bottom edge may extend slightly below the left column content, defining the lower end of the main detail area before the page fades out into additional spacing or any subsequent sections.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right column; on narrow viewports it shifts to a full-width block beneath the other dealer details, maintaining a strong 48–64px separation from the preceding section.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dealer message status appears as a small, floating element anchored to the bottom-right corner of the viewport, visually echoing the mockup’s bottom-right utility button. When visible (e.g., after a message is sent or when an error occurs), it sits above all content, offset approximately 24–32px from the bottom and right edges, and spans a compact width of about 260–320px. It may present as a pill-shaped toast or compact card with concise text, layered on top of the dark background and overlapping the content columns if necessary, but without obscuring important form fields. On longer pages, it remains in a fixed viewport position so that feedback remains immediately visible even when the contact form or hero scroll off-screen.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed-position floating element; always pinned to the viewport’s bottom-right corner when active, with a slight shadow or glow to lift it above the main content and automatically dismissing or retracting after some time.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The global search form is revealed as an overlay panel anchored directly beneath the global header when activated. It is centered within the main content width, spanning about 60% of the content column (roughly 7–8 grid columns) and leaving symmetrical dark margins on both sides for balance. The top edge of the search panel aligns with the bottom edge of the header, and there is 24–32px of padding inside the panel, with the search input itself running nearly full width within this container. When open, it visually sits on top of the dealer hero panel, slightly dimming or subtly de-emphasizing the underlying hero to draw attention to the search interaction while preserving the overall layout structure.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporarily overlaying, positioned as a pseudo-sticky panel under the fixed header; it pushes no content but floats above it, and can slide down/up or fade in/out, always remaining horizontally centered in the content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The global search results panel appears directly beneath the global-search-form overlay, sharing its horizontal alignment and approximate width (~60% of the content column). It forms a vertical dropdown-like panel that overlaps the upper portion of the dealer hero area, with a maximum height of about 50% of the viewport; results inside are scrollable while the rest of the page remains static in the background. The panel’s top is snug to the bottom of the search form, with an 8–16px separation for clarity, and its sides align precisely with the search form edges, creating a visually unified search stack centered on the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Overlay-like, attached to the global-search-form; it scrolls internally while the main page remains fixed in position. It appears and disappears with the search form, not affecting the underlying hero or detail sections’ layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dealer page loading and error state occupies the main hero region directly beneath the header, using the same footprint reserved for the dealer-info-panel. During loading, a centered spinner or progress indicator sits in the middle of this area, with the full width of about 70–80% of the content column and ample dark space around it, keeping the header visible above. For error states, a compact error card with message text is centered within this same region, spanning roughly 40–50% of the content width, again framed by generous margins. Below this hero-sized placeholder, the subsequent sections (services, hours, coverage, contact) are either hidden or visually subdued until data is successfully loaded, so the user’s focus remains on the central status feedback.
