The overlay’s background is slightly translucent or subtly differentiated in tone so that the main page context is perceptible but visually pushed back, keeping focus on the search experience. The floating search control at the bottom-right may remain visible but visually merged into the overlay’s logic, acting as a secondary close/minimize affordance.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Full-screen, fixed-position overlay that sits above the header and all main content. It blocks interaction with underlying components while active and scrolls its own results area independently of the page beneath.

name: About
title: About
description: Page that loads and presents dynamic company information for the CNC manufacturer and provides access to related resources such as training, contact, and global search.

core_features: Load and display company overview, history, mission, locations, certifications, quality standards, and leadership using getAboutPageContent.
Provide a section-based navigation within the About page, optionally controlled via URL parameters (e.g., ?section=leadership).
Offer global search across machines, spare parts, documents, and case studies via the globalSearch interface from the About page.
Allow users to navigate from the About page to the training list (training_list.html) and the contact page (contact.html) via contextual links.
Support deep linking into specific About sections and states using URL parameters such as section and search.
user_workflows: View general company information: 1) User opens about.html with no parameters. 2) The about-content-loader component calls getAboutPageContent. 3) Company overview, history, mission, locations, certifications, quality standards, and leadership sections are populated. 4) User scrolls or uses the section navigation to move between sections.

Deep link to a specific section: 1) User opens about.html?section=locations (or another supported section key). 2) The about-state-manager component reads the section URL parameter. 3) After getAboutPageContent resolves, the relevant section is scrolled into view and highlighted in the section navigation. 4) If the user clicks another section in the navigation, the URL parameter section is updated and the view adjusts accordingly.

Use global search from About page: 1) User enters a search term into the global search input in the header while on about.html. 2) On submit, the about-global-search component calls globalSearch with the current query and selected entityTypes. 3) The search query and entityTypes are reflected in URL parameters (e.g., about.html?search=term&entityTypes=machineModels,documents). 4) The about-search-results-panel component renders categorized results for machineModels, spareParts, documents, and caseStudies. 5) If the user changes the query or clears it, the component updates the URL and re-runs globalSearch or hides results when search is empty.

Navigate from About to training: 1) User reads about the company’s capabilities and wants to learn about training. 2) User clicks a "Training & education" link rendered by the about-related-links component. 3) The click triggers a navigation to training_list.html, optionally including a source=about URL parameter. 4) User continues the journey on the training_list.html page.

Navigate from About to contact: 1) User reads company information and wants to contact the manufacturer. 2) User clicks a "Contact us" link in the related links area or within a leadership section. 3) The click triggers a navigation to contact.html, optionally including a topic=about-company or source=about URL parameter. 4) User can then submit a contact form on contact.html.
interactions: Click on section navigation items (e.g., Overview, History, Mission, Locations, Certifications, Leadership) updates the section URL parameter and scrolls to the selected section.
Page load triggers a data fetch via getAboutPageContent; subsequent reload actions can re-trigger the same interface call.
Typing into the global search input updates local search state; submitting the form triggers globalSearch and updates search-related URL parameters.
Changing entity type filters (e.g., toggling machines, parts, documents, case studies) in the global search UI updates the entityTypes URL parameter and re-calls globalSearch.
Clicking on a search result item can navigate to the corresponding detail or listing page using entity-specific URL patterns (e.g., product detail, document download page, or case study page).
Clicking the "Training & education" link navigates to training_list.html (with optional URL parameters such as source=about).
Clicking the "Contact us" link navigates to contact.html (with optional URL parameters such as topic=about-company or source=about).
page_functionality.state_logic: The About page primarily relies on URL parameters and API responses for its state. Supported URL parameters may include: section – an optional string used to indicate which About section should be focused or scrolled into view (e.g., about.html?section=history). The about-state-manager component reads this parameter on load and when the URL changes, and synchronizes it with the section navigation's active state. search – an optional string used to hold the current global search query when the user searches from within the About page (e.g., about.html?search=cnc). When present and non-empty, about-global-search triggers a call to globalSearch with this query and shows the about-search-results-panel. entityTypes – an optional comma-separated list of entity type identifiers used to filter the globalSearch scope (e.g., about.html?search=cnc&entityTypes=machineModels,documents). If absent, the page uses a default set of entityTypes within the component logic. URL changes initiated by user interactions (section navigation clicks, search submissions, filter toggles) must be reflected in these parameters and should be handled via onUrlChange handlers. No state is stored in localStorage; all state is either derived from URL parameters or from the latest responses from getAboutPageContent and globalSearch.

components.functionality: Fetches and stores About page content by calling getAboutPageContent when the page initializes or when a manual reload is requested. It exposes the resulting data (companyOverview, history, mission, locations, certifications, qualityStandards, leadership) to other components via shared page state.
event_handlers: onInit: Calls getAboutPageContent with no parameters and stores the result in the page state.
onReloadClick: Re-calls getAboutPageContent to refresh the About content.
onLoadSuccess: Notifies dependent components that About content is available.
onLoadError: Updates an error state that downstream components can use to display non-visual functional fallbacks (e.g., disabling internal navigation).

components.functionality: Manages URL-parameter-driven state for the About page, such as the active section and search query. It synchronizes the section URL parameter with the currently focused About section and ensures that components react to URL changes.
event_handlers: onInit: Reads initial URL parameters (section, search, entityTypes) and exposes them to other components as initial state.
onUrlChange: Listens to changes in the browser URL and updates the active section and search-related state when parameters change.
onSectionChangeRequest: Receives requests from section navigation or other components to change the active section; updates URL.section accordingly.
onSearchStateChangeRequest: Receives search query and entityTypes changes from the about-global-search component; updates URL.search and URL.entityTypes accordingly.

components.functionality: Provides an internal navigation interface to jump between About sections (overview, history, mission, locations, certifications, leadership). It updates and responds to the section URL parameter so that the active section is reflected in the URL and vice versa.
event_handlers: onInit: Reads URL.section via about-state-manager to set the initially active navigation item.
onSectionNavClick: When a user clicks a section item, notifies about-state-manager to set URL.section to the corresponding section key and triggers scrolling/focusing of that section.
onActiveSectionChange: When section changes (e.g., from URL updates or user scrolling), updates the active item state so navigation reflects the focused section.

components.functionality: Displays the company overview, mission, and history text fields returned by getAboutPageContent. It can react to the active section state to expand, collapse, or scroll into view when the section parameter targets overview, mission, or history.
event_handlers: onContentLoad: Renders the overview, mission, and history content when getAboutPageContent data becomes available.
onSectionFocusRequest: When the active section (from URL.section) matches any of the subsection identifiers that belong here, this component scrolls into view or updates internal focus state.
onInternalAnchorClick: If the component exposes internal anchors (e.g., a link to jump from overview to mission), it triggers about-state-manager to update URL.section accordingly.

components.functionality: Lists manufacturing and office locations using the locations array from getAboutPageContent, showing each entry’s name and address. It responds to section-based focusing triggered by the URL.
event_handlers: onContentLoad: Renders the list of locations once getAboutPageContent.locations is available.
onSectionFocusRequest: If URL.section equals the locations section key, this component scrolls or focuses the locations list.

onLocationItemInteraction: Handles generic interactions on a location entry (e.g., if an address link updates the browser’s address bar for mapping, it can trigger an external navigation without affecting About page state).

components.functionality: Displays the list of certifications and the quality standards description from getAboutPageContent, providing users with information about the company’s quality framework.
event_handlers: onContentLoad: Renders certifications and quality standards text once available.
onSectionFocusRequest: If URL.section matches the certifications section key, ensures this section is focused.

onCertificationItemInteraction: Can handle non-visual functional actions such as triggering navigation to a general documents or certifications explanation page if such links are provided by the content (link URLs themselves should come from dynamic content, not hardcoded).

components.functionality: Displays leadership entries based on the leadership array returned by getAboutPageContent, including each leader’s name and role. It may offer contextual navigation to the contact page for leadership-related inquiries.
event_handlers: onContentLoad: Renders the leadership list when getAboutPageContent.leadership is available.
onSectionFocusRequest: If URL.section targets the leadership section key, scrolls or focuses this component.

onContactLeadershipClick: When a user chooses to contact leadership or a general contact link within this section, navigates to contact.html with an optional topic=about-company or source=leadership parameter.

components.functionality: Provides contextual links from the About page to related site areas, specifically training_list.html and contact.html, using URL-based navigation.
event_handlers: onTrainingLinkClick: Navigates to training_list.html, optionally appending a source=about URL parameter to indicate origin.
onContactLinkClick: Navigates to contact.html, optionally appending topic=about-company or source=about to help the contact page pre-select a relevant topic.

onSectionContextChange: Can adjust which links are emphasized based on URL.section (for example, highlighting training when viewing sections related to skills or education if such mapping is defined in configuration, not hardcoded in the component).

components.functionality: Implements the global search input and optional entity-type filters when the user performs a search from the About page. It calls the globalSearch interface and shares results with the about-search-results-panel component while maintaining search state in the URL.
event_handlers: onInit: Reads URL.search and URL.entityTypes to pre-populate the search input and filter selection, and if search is present and non-empty, triggers a globalSearch call.
onInputChange: Updates local search query state as the user types; may delay calling globalSearch until form submission or debounced triggers.
onEntityTypeToggle: Updates selected entity types used for search; requests about-state-manager to update URL.entityTypes and re-runs globalSearch with the new entityTypes set.
onSubmit: Calls globalSearch with the current query and entityTypes, then updates URL.search and URL.entityTypes via about-state-manager.
onClearSearch: Clears the search field, resets related URL parameters (removing search and entityTypes if needed), and hides or clears the about-search-results-panel.

components.functionality: Displays categorized search results for machine models, spare parts, downloadable documents, and case studies returned by the globalSearch interface when a search is active on the About page.
event_handlers: onResultsUpdate: Renders or updates result lists whenever a new globalSearch response is received.

onResultItemClick: Navigates to the appropriate detail or listing page for the selected result using a URL pattern appropriate to the entity type (for example, product detail for MachineModel, spare part detail, document download page, or case study detail page), passing the relevant id as a URL parameter.

onSearchParamChange: When URL.search or URL.entityTypes changes (e.g., via back/forward navigation), ensures that displayed results correspond to the current parameters or hides itself if search is not set or empty.

layout.grid_system: Likely a centered fixed-width grid (approximately 1140–1200px max-width) on a full-bleed dark background, conceptually compatible with a 12-column grid with wide outer gutters.
layout.layout_pattern: The About page keeps the same full-width dark shell with a fixed-height header and a large cinematic hero at the top. All About-related components live in a centered content column beneath the hero, stacked in a linear top-to-bottom order: section navigation just below the hero, then the company overview as the first major section, followed by locations, certifications, leadership, and finally a related links section near the bottom. Global search is treated as a utility floating above this stack at the bottom-right of the viewport, and the search results appear in a sliding panel from the right when invoked. Data loader and state manager are non-visual and overlay or orchestrate the main content area without changing the visible structure.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The About page keeps the same full-width dark shell with a fixed-height header and a large cinematic hero at the top. All About-related components live in a centered content column beneath the hero, stacked in a linear top-to-bottom order: section navigation just below the hero, then the company overview as the first major section, followed by locations, certifications, leadership, and finally a related links section near the bottom. Global search is treated as a utility floating above this stack at the bottom-right of the viewport, and the search results appear in a sliding panel from the right when invoked. Data loader and state manager are non-visual and overlay or orchestrate the main content area without changing the visible structure.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The main content sits in a centered column with a max-width of roughly 1140–1200px, inset on both sides by generous dark margins. Each major section (overview, locations, certifications, leadership, related links) occupies about 70–80% of the page width within this column, leaving 10–15% of the page width as breathing room on each side. Vertical spacing between sections is generous, in the 48–64px range, preserving the premium, low-density feel. The header uses about 10–15% of the viewport height; the hero occupies the majority of the first viewport. The floating global search trigger and its results panel consume minimal footprint when idle, but the results panel, when open, takes around 30–35% of the viewport width anchored to the right edge, overlaying the underlying content.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content is intentionally low-density. Each section has a clear title, ample negative space around text blocks, and comfortable line lengths, with lists laid out in 2–3 columns rather than dense single-column stacks. The design avoids crowding multiple functions together: section navigation is in its own horizontal band, content sections are separated with generous vertical gaps, and the global search utility is isolated at the bottom-right as a single, compact call-to-action. Lists like locations, certifications, and leadership are grouped into visually clear clusters or cards with consistent spacing, following an 8px-based rhythm.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The user’s eye follows an extended F/Z-pattern: starting at the yellow logo in the top-left of the header, scanning across the navigation to the top-right, then dropping diagonally to the center hero image. From there, the eye continues downward to the section navigation bar just below the hero, then scans horizontally across the section tabs. The flow then drops into the first major content block (company overview) and continues down the vertical stack of sections. As the user scrolls, the sticky section navigation remains at the top edge of the content to support orientation, and the bright global search button in the bottom-right acts as the secondary attractor at the end of the reading path, with the search results panel appearing from the right side when engaged.

layout.overall_description: The About page sits inside a full-bleed charcoal background with a fixed-height top header and a wide, cinematic hero image that showcases the CNC machinery, mirroring the industrial, premium aesthetic. Beneath the hero, the main About content is centered in a max-width column, leaving broad dark margins on both sides so that each section appears as a focused, well-framed island. A slim, typography-led section navigation strip runs horizontally just below the hero, aligned with the content column, offering anchors to Overview, Locations, Certifications, and Leadership; this band becomes sticky as the user scrolls so it always hovers at the top of the content area. The company overview section follows immediately below as the first large content block, with ample padding and a balanced layout that might split into a narrative column and a supporting facts/metrics column. Below it, the Locations, Certifications, and Leadership sections stack vertically, each occupying the same centered width and separated by generous vertical gaps, with list items laid out in low-density grids or rows that feel technical but refined. The page concludes with a Related Links section that offers pathways to training, contact, and other resources, framed as a clean, multi-column list with subtle dividers. Throughout, a compact, high-contrast search trigger hovers at the bottom-right of the viewport; when activated, a dark search panel and results list slides in from the right, partially overlaying the main content while maintaining the strong lateral margins and cinematic feel. Non-visual infrastructure like the data loader and state manager orchestrate the content transitions, showing a subtle full-content-area loading overlay when needed without disrupting the underlying grid or spatial calm.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The content loader occupies the same centered content column used by all About sections, beginning just below the hero and section navigation and extending down to cover the full height of the About content stack. When active, it presents as a semi-transparent dark overlay that spans roughly 70–80% of the page width (matching the content width) and the visible height of the About content area, leaving the outer side margins in the darker shell still visible. A minimal, centrally aligned loading indicator sits in the vertical and horizontal center of this overlay, keeping the focus tight and aligned with the hero’s central framing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears as an absolute-positioned overlay anchored to the top-left of the main About content area (just below the section navigation), stretching to its right and bottom edges. It scrolls with the page content and disappears when loading is complete, revealing the underlying stacked sections.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The state manager is non-visual and does not occupy explicit space in the layout. Conceptually, it sits behind the entire About content stack, controlling which sections are visible, when the loader overlay displays, and whether the search results panel is open. It has no direct visual footprint and does not affect spacing or alignment.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendered logic layer with no positioning; it influences the state of other components (loader, section navigation active state, search panel) without modifying the layout grid.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The section navigation is a slim horizontal band positioned directly below the hero image, centered within the main content column. It spans roughly 70–80% of the page width, aligned with the left and right edges of the content sections, and sits with about 48px of vertical space separating it from the bottom of the hero. Within this band, 4–5 text-based navigation items (e.g., Overview, Locations, Certifications, Leadership) are laid out in a single row from left to right, with equal spacing and a subtle emphasis on the active section via a thin underline or a saturated yellow label. There is approximately 24–32px of padding above and below the nav items, giving it enough visual weight to delineate the start of the About content without feeling bulky.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a sticky element: when the user scrolls past its initial position, it pins to the top of the viewport just below the fixed site header, maintaining its full width within the central content column. It remains fixed in this position while the underlying sections scroll, reinforcing orientation across the long, vertical page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The company overview section is the first major content block in the About area, positioned directly below the section navigation with a vertical gap of about 32–40px. It occupies roughly 70–80% of the page width, centered in the viewport, leaving consistent dark side margins on both sides. The section has a strong, left-aligned heading at the top, followed by content that likely splits into two columns on larger screens: the left two-thirds for narrative text (company story, mission, value proposition) and the right one-third for key facts or metrics, each column padded with 24–32px internal spacing to maintain a light, breathable feel. The section itself has generous top and bottom padding (48–64px) so that it feels like a defined, self-contained block, visually echoing the proportions and framing of the hero but in text form.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrolls naturally with the page. On narrower viewports, the columns collapse into a single column while preserving the same outer margins and vertical spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The locations section sits directly beneath the company overview, separated by approximately 48–64px of vertical whitespace. It shares the same centered width (about 70–80% of the page width) and alignment, creating a consistent vertical spine. A bold section heading appears at the top left, followed by a grid or list of locations laid out in 2–3 columns depending on screen width, each location block sized to maintain comfortable spacing (16–24px gaps horizontally and vertically) between items. The vertical span of this section is determined by the number of locations but retains a clear top and bottom boundary with 32–48px internal padding, ensuring that the list feels organized and not crowded against the surrounding sections.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, part of the vertical scroll. Its top edge remains aligned to the content column; the section navigation above continues to stick to the top of the viewport while this section scrolls underneath.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The certifications section is stacked below the locations section, with a vertical gap of roughly 48px to maintain separation. It keeps the same centered width and lateral margins as the other sections. At the top, a left-aligned heading is followed by a horizontally arranged set of certification items—such as logos, badges, or short textual labels—laid out in one or two rows. Each certification item has ample padding (12–16px) and an 8–16px gap between items, possibly aligned in a row of compact cards or pill-like elements to echo the technical, precise character of the brand. The overall block has slightly less vertical depth than the locations and leadership sections, making it feel like a concise, high-credibility strip in the middle of the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrolls with the page. It aligns visually and structurally with the other sections, maintaining consistent top and bottom padding and the same left/right boundaries of the content grid.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The leadership section appears below the certifications section with about 48–64px of vertical spacing, again centered and occupying roughly 70–80% of the page width. A prominent section title at the top introduces a grid of leadership profiles, typically laid out as 2–3 columns of cards containing a portrait, name, and title. Each card is given generous internal padding (16–24px) and a vertical rhythm of 8–16px between text elements, with 24–32px of space between cards, which maintains the low-density aesthetic while supporting more visually rich content. The section may be one of the taller blocks on the page due to the image content, but the consistent side margins and a clear bottom boundary (with 48px of padding below the last row of profiles) keep it visually anchored within the vertical stack.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static in the scroll. The layout remains grid-based on larger screens and collapses to a single-column stack on smaller devices, while preserving the same center alignment and generous spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The related links component sits near the bottom of the About page, following the leadership section with a vertical gap of approximately 64px to mark it as the concluding utility area. It shares the same centered width as the other sections but feels slightly more compact vertically. A subtle top divider line or tonal shift in the background may separate it from the section above, reinforcing its role as a footer-like navigation block. Inside, a short label (e.g., Related Resources) appears at the top left, with related links (such as Training, Contact, Support, Documentation) arranged in 2–3 columns or a horizontal row, each link given generous horizontal padding and 8–16px spacing between items. The block has 32–48px of padding above and below the links, ensuring they feel deliberate and easy to scan without crowding the page’s visual endpoint.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static at the bottom of the scrollable content. It anchors the end of the About content stack before any global site footer, maintaining the same alignment and margins as the other sections.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The global search component manifests as a compact floating control at the bottom-right corner of the viewport, visually echoing the floating button observed in the mockup. In its idle state, it appears as a small circular or pill-shaped button with a magnifying glass icon (and optional short label), in the saturated brand yellow, positioned approximately 24–32px away from both the right and bottom edges of the viewport. Its size is modest—just large enough to be easily clickable—so it does not compete with the main content but remains clearly discoverable in the user’s peripheral vision as they scroll through the About sections.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed to the viewport, overlaying all page content. It stays anchored to the bottom-right corner regardless of scroll position. When activated, it either expands in place into a small right-aligned input field or triggers the full search results panel that slides in from the right, while the button itself remains visible as the primary search control.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The search results panel occupies a vertical slice along the right side of the viewport when the global search is active. It slides in from the right edge and spans roughly 30–35% of the viewport width, from just below the fixed header down to a point slightly above the floating search button (maintaining a 16–24px margin at the bottom). The panel has a dark, matte background with subtle inner padding (24–32px on all sides), and contains the search input at the top followed by a vertically scrollable list of results. Its left edge overlaps the main content column, partially covering sections like Overview or Locations while leaving a visible portion of the underlying page on the left, preserving the sense of depth and the central alignment of the main content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed overlay that slides in from the right side of the viewport and remains fixed while the underlying page content is scroll-locked or dimmed. It aligns its top edge just underneath the site header, respects the bottom offset where the floating button sits, and can be dismissed to slide back out of view, restoring the full-width view of the About sections.

name: Contact
title: Contact
description: Page for general inquiries with a contact form, company contact details, guidance to other support flows, and optional map to the main office.

core_features: Load and display headquarters address, phone numbers, and general email addresses via getContactPageContent.
Provide a general contact form for sales, support, media, and other inquiries, submitted via submitContactForm.
Use optional URL parameters (topic, sourcePage) to preselect the inquiry topic and include routing context in the submitted message.
Display usage guidance explaining when to use the dealer locator, spare parts, or service request flows, using usageGuidance from getContactPageContent.
Provide navigation guidance to the dealer locator page (dealer_locator.html) from the Contact page.
Optionally render a simple office/showroom map using the headquartersAddress from getContactPageContent as the map location.
Allow users to perform a global search from this page using the globalSearch interface.
user_workflows:
General inquiry via direct navigation:
1. User opens contact.html without URL parameters.
2. Page-init logic calls getContactPageContent and populates address, phone numbers, email addresses, and usageGuidance.
3. User reviews contact information and guidance.
4. User fills in the contact form fields (name, email, topic, phone, message, preferredContactMethod).
5. User submits the form, triggering submitContactForm with the entered values.
6. Page displays the feedback message and success/failure state based on submitContactForm response.

Guided inquiry from another page with preselected topic:
1. User clicks a "Contact" link on cart.html, about.html, or index.html that navigates to contact.html with a topic URL parameter (e.g., contact.html?topic=sales).
2. On load, the Contact page reads the topic URL parameter.
3. If the topic value matches an allowed topic (e.g., sales, support, media), the topic field in the contact form is preselected accordingly.
4. User reviews preselected topic, completes remaining required fields, and submits the form.
5. The form submission calls submitContactForm, passing the selected topic along with name, email, phone, message, and preferredContactMethod.
6. User sees confirmation or error feedback from the API response.

Inquiry with internal routing context from transactional pages:
1. User navigates from cart.html, maintenance_plan_detail.html, or training_detail.html to contact.html with both topic and sourcePage URL parameters (e.g., contact.html?topic=support&sourcePage=maintenance_plan_detail).
2. Page-init logic reads sourcePage and stores it in the page state (not persisted beyond this session).
3. The form component shows a contextual note (e.g., that the inquiry is related to a cart, maintenance plan, or training) based on sourcePage.
4. When the user submits the form, the form logic appends a routing note derived from sourcePage to the message field (e.g., includes a line indicating the sourcePage value) before calling submitContactForm.
5. submitContactForm is called with this enriched message to support internal routing by the back-office systems.
6. User sees the feedback message returned by submitContactForm.

Guidance to dealer locator or other flows:
1. User lands on contact.html and reads usageGuidance content which explains when to use dealer locator, spare parts, or service request flows.
2. If the user determines they need a dealer instead of general contact, they click the "Find a dealer" guidance link in the guidance section.
3. The page navigates to dealer_locator.html without additional parameters (or with parameters if later extended), as defined by the outgoing connection.
4. The user continues their journey on the dealer locator page.

Using global search from the Contact page:
1. User types a search query into the global search bar available on this page.
2. The global search component calls globalSearch with the query and relevant entityTypes (e.g., machine_models, spare_parts, documents, case_studies).
3. Search results are displayed (e.g., in a dropdown or overlay) grouped by entity type.
4. User clicks a search result and is navigated to the corresponding detail page using the relevant URL pattern (e.g., product or document detail pages), with the Contact page remaining only as the origin.
interactions:
Page initialization:
- On initial load of contact.html, the page reads URL parameters topic and sourcePage.
- The page triggers getContactPageContent once and binds the returned headquartersAddress, phoneNumbers, emailAddresses, and usageGuidance to respective components.
- The contact form component initializes its topic field based on the topic URL parameter when present and valid.

Topic selection in the contact form:
- User interacts with a topic selector (e.g., dropdown) offering topics such as sales, support, media, and possibly others.
- Changing the topic updates the internal form state; it does not modify the URL unless explicitly designed to do so.
- If the user changes the topic manually, this selected value overrides any initial preselection based on the URL topic parameter for the current session.

Form input and validation:
- User fills in required fields (name, email, topic, message) and optional fields (phone, preferredContactMethod).
- On input blur or form submission, client-side validation checks for required fields and simple format validation (e.g., email format).
- Validation errors are indicated per field, and submission is blocked until required fields are valid.
- No static placeholder content is assumed; labels and messages are either static UI text or driven by configuration, not by API content.

Form submission via submitContactForm:
- On clicking the submit button, if validation passes, the form component constructs the payload for submitContactForm.
- If a sourcePage URL parameter is present, the form logic appends a note containing the sourcePage value to the message field before submission for internal routing purposes.
- submitContactForm is called with name, email, topic, phone, the final message string, and preferredContactMethod.
- While awaiting the response, the submit button is disabled and a loading state is shown.
- After receiving the response, if success is true, the component displays the feedback message and may clear or lock the form; if success is false, the component displays the feedback message as an error and re-enables the form.

Guidance link interactions:
- User clicks on a "Find a dealer" link or button within the guidance section.
- The click event triggers navigation to dealer_locator.html as defined by the outgoing connection.
- Additional guidance links (if present) may be simple anchors to other internal pages (e.g., spare parts or service request pages) following their respective URL schemas.

Map interaction:
- If a map is enabled, the map component uses the headquartersAddress from getContactPageContent to build a map embed URL for an external map provider or a link that opens in a new tab.
- User can click on the map or a link near the map to open a larger map view in a new browser tab or window.
- No hardcoded coordinates or addresses are used; all location data is obtained dynamically via getContactPageContent.

Global search usage:
- User types into the global search field and submits the query (e.g., via Enter key or a search button).
- The global search component calls globalSearch with the query and a defined set of entityTypes.
- When results are returned, the component displays them grouped by entity type.
- Clicking any result triggers navigation to the corresponding detail page with appropriate URL parameters; the Contact page does not handle those details beyond exiting to the selected page.
page_functionality.state_logic: The Contact page state is primarily driven by URL parameters and API responses, without using localStorage. On every page load, the browser URL is parsed for `topic` and `sourcePage` query parameters. If `topic` is present and its value matches a recognized topic (such as 'sales', 'support', or 'media'), the contact form initializes its topic field to that value; otherwise, it falls back to a neutral default topic state. The `sourcePage` parameter, when present (e.g., values like 'cart', 'maintenance_plan_detail', 'training_detail'), is stored in in-memory page state for the current session and is not persisted across reloads. This `sourcePage` value influences: (1) a contextual note shown near the contact form to remind the user what context their inquiry will reference, and (2) the final message payload sent to `submitContactForm`, where a routing note derived from `sourcePage` is appended to the message string before submission. The page also maintains transient UI states: loading and error state for `getContactPageContent`, loading and success/error state for `submitContactForm`, and any active global search query/results. These states are reset on full page reloads. Navigation from this page to `dealer_locator.html` occurs through user interaction with guidance links and does not carry additional URL parameters by default, although the implementation can later be extended to add parameters if required, as long as it does not conflict with the defined outgoing connection. All contact details and guidance text are retrieved dynamically via `getContactPageContent`, and no static address, phone, or email values are embedded in the page logic.

components.functionality: Handles initial page setup: parses URL parameters, calls getContactPageContent, initializes global state for contact details, usage guidance, and form defaults.
Displays dynamic headquarters address, phone numbers, and general email addresses retrieved via getContactPageContent.
Shows usage guidance text explaining when to use the contact form versus dealer locator, spare parts, or service request flows and exposes a link to dealer_locator.html.
Provides a general inquiry form with fields for name, email, topic, phone, message, and preferredContactMethod, integrating URL topic/sourcePage and submitting via submitContactForm.
Uses the headquartersAddress from getContactPageContent to construct and render an embedded map or map link to the main office or showroom.

Displays a small contextual note above or near the contact form when a sourcePage URL parameter is present, indicating the context of the inquiry (e.g., related to a cart, maintenance plan, or training course).
Provides a global search input that allows users to search across machine models, spare parts, documents, and case studies from the Contact page.
Displays grouped search results for machineModels, spareParts, documents, and caseStudies returned by globalSearch when a user searches from the Contact page.

layout.grid_system: Likely a centered fixed-width grid (approximately 1140–1200px max-width) on a full-bleed dark background, conceptually compatible with a 12-column grid with wide outer gutters.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom structure: a fixed-height dark header with logo/navigation (site-level), then a large cinematic hero image focused on CNC machinery as the first major content element. The contact-specific components begin directly below the hero, stacked vertically in clearly separated bands. The primary contact interaction (contact-form) is placed in a two-column band paired with the contact-info-panel, followed by a full-width guidance section and then the office map. Utilities (global-search-bar and its results) behave as floating/overlaid elements tied to the header’s right side, echoing the mockup’s floating utility button pattern. The source-context-banner sits just above the main contact section, acting as a slim contextual layer between hero and form/content.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom structure: a fixed-height dark header with logo/navigation (site-level), then a large cinematic hero image focused on CNC machinery as the first major content element. The contact-specific components begin directly below the hero, stacked vertically in clearly separated bands. The primary contact interaction (contact-form) is placed in a two-column band paired with the contact-info-panel, followed by a full-width guidance section and then the office map. Utilities (global-search-bar and its results) behave as floating/overlaid elements tied to the header’s right side, echoing the mockup’s floating utility button pattern. The source-context-banner sits just above the main contact section, acting as a slim contextual layer between hero and form/content.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The layout uses a centered content column with a max width of about 1140–1200px inside a full-bleed charcoal-dark background. Left and right margins (gutter space) are roughly 10–15% of the viewport width on large screens, creating strong lateral whitespace. The header occupies about 10–15% of the viewport height; the hero image below it takes up the majority of the initial viewport (around 55–65%), inset from the viewport edges by 40–64px. The primary contact section (info + form) occupies the next significant vertical band, with 48–64px vertical spacing between major sections (hero → banner → contact section → guidance → map). Individual components have generous padding (16–24px) inside their containers, maintaining an airy, premium feel.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density remains low and focused. Above the fold, the only substantial content is the hero visual and minimal page title, with utilities staying subtle. Below, the contact area presents a limited number of clearly grouped elements: a compact, text-focused contact-info-panel, a clean, vertically stacked contact-form with ample input spacing, and concise guidance text blocks. The office map is given room to breathe with surrounding dark space. Overall, typography and negative space do the heavy lifting—no overcrowded sections, no more than 2–3 focal elements per horizontal band.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow follows the mockup’s F/Z pattern: the eye begins at the bright industrial-yellow logo in the header’s top-left, moves horizontally across the navigation to the top-right where the global-search-bar sits as a subtle utility. From there, attention drops diagonally down to the center of the hero image. As the user scrolls, the eye lands on the source-context-banner as a slim contextual cue, then continues into the main contact band where the contact-form (right, larger) and contact-info-panel (left, denser) form a secondary Z: left panel → across to the larger form → down to the guidance section → across to the map. The global-search-results, when open, temporarily intercept this flow as a right-aligned overlay beneath the search bar, consistent with the mockup’s concept of floating utilities above the main content.

layout.overall_description: The Contact page sits inside a full-width, charcoal-dark shell with a fixed-height industrial header at the top and a centered content column below. A wide, cinematic hero image of CNC machinery spans roughly 70–80% of the content width under the header, inset from the page edges to create thick dark margins and instant focus. Below this hero, the main contact content begins in a vertical stack: a slim contextual banner; a dominant two-column band where the right side is a generous, clean contact form and the left side is a compact block of company contact details; a full-width guidance section that reads as a calm, text-led layer; and a wide office map embedded near the bottom, floating within the dark background. Overlaid on the top-right, aligned with the header/navigation, the global search bar sits as a slim utility that, when engaged, drops a right-aligned results panel over the upper portion of the page without disturbing the main layout. The overall impression is of a modern, industrial, premium interface where each section is clearly delineated by space and alignment, with the contact form and information occupying center stage after the hero visual has done its brand-setting work.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component is non-visual and sits logically behind all other elements. It is triggered as soon as the Contact page route is loaded. It does not occupy any specific space in the grid or alter the visible layout; instead, it prepares data, context, or configuration before the header, hero, and contact components are rendered into the main centered content column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual logic component; no spatial footprint and no positional behavior in the layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact-info-panel appears in the first major content band below the hero and source-context-banner, positioned on the left side of a two-column layout. Within the centered content area (approximately 1140–1200px wide), it occupies about one-third of the width (roughly 4 of 12 grid columns), starting flush with the left inner edge of the content column. Vertically, its top edge aligns with the top of the contact-form on the right, forming a single cohesive band. The panel itself is a vertically oriented card with a slightly lighter dark background than the page shell, with 24px internal padding on all sides and clear 16–24px spacing between text items like address, phone, and email. It extends downwards to accommodate its content, typically shorter than the form, creating a visually balanced yet clearly subordinate block on the left. There is 48–64px of dark vertical space separating this whole band from the source-context-banner above, and another 48–64px before the guidance section below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level element inside the main grid; on narrower screens it would drop below the contact-form and expand to near full width, but on large screens it remains locked to the left third of the primary contact band.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact-guidance-section sits directly below the main contact band (contact-info-panel and contact-form), separated by a generous vertical gap of about 48–64px to maintain the low density aesthetic. It spans the full width of the centered content column (roughly 70–80% of the viewport, respecting the wide outer gutters), forming a single horizontal stripe in the layout. Within this stripe, its content—short paragraphs or segmented guidance blocks—is arranged in one or two text columns, but visually still reads as a single full-width band. The section height is determined by the text, with ample 24px padding top and bottom and 16–24px line spacing, ensuring the content feels airy, not crowded. Dark background tone remains consistent with the rest of the page, with subtle contrast (e.g., a slightly lighter panel or rule lines) distinguishing guidance segments without introducing clutter.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard flow element; it scrolls with the page and maintains its position beneath the contact band. On smaller screens, any internal multi-column text collapses into a single vertical stack while preserving the same top/bottom spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact-form is the primary interactive element on the page and shares the first major content band with the contact-info-panel. Within the centered content column, it occupies roughly two-thirds of the width (about 8 of 12 grid columns), positioned immediately to the right of the contact-info-panel with a clear 24–32px horizontal gap between them. Its left edge lines up with the vertical grid column next to the info panel; its right edge aligns with the right inner boundary of the content column, leaving about 10–15% viewport margin to the outer page edge. Vertically, the top of the form aligns with the top of the info panel, creating a unified horizontal band. The form itself is a tall, clean card with a slightly lighter dark background, 24px padding on all sides, and form fields stacked vertically with 16–24px spacing between each label-input pair. The submit button is anchored near the bottom-right of the form area, with around 24px margin from the form’s edges, enhancing the right-weighted visual pull consistent with the Z-pattern. Overall, the form’s height typically exceeds that of the info panel, giving it visual dominance in the band while keeping a balanced composition.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level element in the main grid. On smaller screens, it shifts to occupy nearly 100% of the width above the contact-info-panel, but on large screens it remains right-aligned and paired horizontally with the info panel.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The office-map-embed is positioned as the final major content section near the bottom of the page, below the contact-guidance-section. There is a generous 48–64px vertical gap between the bottom of the guidance content and the top edge of the map, allowing it to feel like a distinct, visual closure to the page. The map itself is centered within the content column and spans slightly less than full content width—about 70–80% of the centered column—to preserve a frame of dark space on both sides, mirroring the hero image’s inset framing. Its height is fixed in a cinematic rectangle (approximately 320–400px tall), giving it substantial but controlled presence. The embed appears inside a subtle card container with minimal or no border, slightly lighter than the background, with 16–24px padding to avoid the map touching container edges. Below the map, there is another 48–64px of dark breathing room before the page footer, keeping the layout feeling open.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrollable element within the normal document flow. The map remains fixed within its section but is fully responsive, shrinking in width on narrower screens while maintaining its central alignment and aspect ratio.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The source-context-banner is a slim, horizontal strip that sits at the top of the main contact content area, directly below the hero image and its lower margin. There is a 32–48px gap between the bottom of the hero and the top edge of this banner to keep the hero’s visual impact intact while clearly signaling the transition into interactive content. The banner spans the full width of the centered content column (aligned with both the info panel and the contact form edges), but is relatively shallow in height—roughly 48–64px including padding. Its background is slightly lighter than the page shell but more subdued than the cards for the form, making it feel informational rather than interactive. Text is centered vertically within the strip, with 16–24px left and right padding and perhaps aligned to the left within the content column. This placement ensures that any message about how the user arrived here (e.g., from a product or support page) is visible immediately as the contact section begins, without competing visually with the hero or the main form.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard in-flow element; it scrolls away with the content. On very tall viewports, it may appear near the bottom of the hero’s visible area, but it never overlaps the hero; it always sits just below, in the main column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The global-search-bar is positioned near the top-right of the page, visually integrated with the header/navigation area rather than the main contact content. On large screens, it sits horizontally aligned with the navigation links and language selector, anchored to the right edge of the centered content column, about 16–24px below the top of the header’s visible area or inline with the nav elements depending on implementation. It is a relatively low-profile, horizontal field, approximately 280–360px wide and 40–48px tall, with subtle styling that complements the industrial theme—flat, minimal, and typography-led. The search bar’s right edge aligns with the right edge of the content column, keeping it within the same vertical grid as the contact-form below. There is clear negative space above and below (16–24px) so it doesn’t feel cramped against the header or the hero. In the visual reading order, it is discovered as the eye scans from the left logo across the navigation to the right.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Semi-fixed utility element: it is anchored within the header region and remains visible at the top when the page is loaded. If the header is sticky, the search bar remains in view as the user scrolls; otherwise, it scrolls away with the header. It serves as the anchor for the global-search-results overlay, which appears directly beneath it.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The global-search-results component appears only when the global-search-bar is engaged. It drops down from the bottom edge of the search bar, aligned to its left and right edges, creating a right-aligned overlay panel that floats above the hero or upper contact content. The panel width matches or slightly exceeds the search bar (for example, 320–400px wide), and it extends downward up to about 50–60% of the viewport height, with internal scrolling if there are many results. This overlay panel uses a slightly lighter dark background for contrast and has 16–24px padding around the result items, which are stacked vertically with 8–16px separation. It does not push the main page content down; instead, it overlays it, maintaining the underlying hero or contact sections visible around its edges, similar to the mockup’s floating utility button behavior but in a top-right configuration. A subtle shadow or glow distinguishes it from the content beneath without breaking the minimal aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Floating overlay anchored to the global-search-bar. Positioned absolutely or fixed relative to the header region: when open, it remains attached to the search bar’s position (just below it), even as the user scrolls a small amount if the header is sticky. Outside clicks or search dismissal hide the panel, returning full focus to the underlying hero and contact content.
